name: Alto de Aguafría – circular from the Cruz del Puerto recreational area
name_original: Alto de Aguafría – circular desde el área recreativa Cruz del Puerto
region_intro: Hiking around Monesterio offers diverse landscapes within the Extremadura region, situated at the foothills of the Sierra Morena. The area features extensive dehesas characterized by oak and cork oak trees, alongside mountainous terrain including the Sierra de Monesterio, Sierra del Alto de Aguafría, and Sierra de Tentudía. These natural features provide varied elevations and environments for outdoor activities. Small lagoons are also present amidst the pastures, contributing to the region's natural composition. Best hiking trails in Monesterio The most popular hiking route is Alto de Aguafría – circular from the Cruz del Puerto recreational area, a 6.8 miles (11.0 km) trail that takes 3 hours 19 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers panoramic views from the Sierra del Alto de Aguafría. Another top favourite among local hikers is Castillo de Aguafria loop from Monesterio, a moderate 9.5 miles (15.3 km) path. This trail explores the area around the Sierra del Alto de Aguafría, providing varied terrain. Local hikers also love the Cerro de Tentudia – Monastery of Tentudía loop from Monesterio, a 10.4 miles (16.8 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain, often completed in about 5 hours 2 minutes. This difficult route includes an ascent to Cerro de Tentudía. Hiking around Monesterio is defined by dehesas, Sierra Morena foothills, and oak and cork oak forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to more challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Monesterio are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from around 35 reviews. More than 190 hikers have used komoot to explore Monesterio's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking around Monesterio?
A: Monesterio, nestled in the Extremadura region, offers a rich variety of landscapes. You'll encounter extensive &lt;strong&gt;Dehesas&lt;/strong&gt;, characterized by pastures dotted with oak and cork oak trees, providing picturesque and gentle hiking stages. The area is also mountainous, with prominent features like the Sierra de Monesterio, Sierra del Alto de Aguafría, and the Sierra de Tentudía, which includes the highest point in Badajoz province, Cerro de Tentudía. Small lagoons can also be found amidst the pastures, adding to the natural beauty.

Q: How many hiking trails are available in Monesterio?
A: There are over 15 hiking routes available around Monesterio, catering to various skill levels. These include easy strolls through the dehesas, moderate paths with varied terrain, and more challenging mountain ascents.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly hiking options in Monesterio?
A: Yes, Monesterio offers several easy routes suitable for families or those looking for a relaxed walk. There are 4 easy trails available, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14621157" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiking loop from Monesterio&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 6.4 km and takes approximately 2 hours to complete.

Q: What historical or natural landmarks can I explore along the trails?
A: Monesterio's trails often lead to fascinating landmarks. You can discover the ruins of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3754437" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of the Towers&lt;/a&gt;, a late medieval strong house, or visit the significant &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3164075" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tentudía Summit&lt;/a&gt;. For those interested in ancient history, the 5,000-year-old &lt;strong&gt;Dolmen of the Goat&lt;/strong&gt; is located near the Vereda de la Plata. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5392543" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rivera de Cala&lt;/a&gt; offers a natural point of interest.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Monesterio?
A: Yes, Monesterio features several popular circular routes. For panoramic views, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991577337/alto-de-aguafria-circular-from-the-cruz-del-puerto-recreational-area" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Aguafría – circular from the Cruz del Puerto recreational area&lt;/a&gt;. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6887439" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Monesterio – Sierra de Monesterio loop from Monesterio&lt;/a&gt;, offering scenic vistas of the town and surrounding sierra.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Monesterio?
A: Given Monesterio's diverse landscapes, including mountainous areas and dehesas, spring and autumn are generally ideal for hiking. The temperatures are milder, and the natural scenery is vibrant, making for comfortable and enjoyable treks. Summer can be hot, while winter offers cooler, crisp conditions.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Monesterio?
A: The hiking routes in Monesterio are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.6 stars from around 35 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, the beautiful dehesa landscapes, and the rewarding views from the higher points like the Sierra del Alto de Aguafría.

Q: Can I find challenging hikes in Monesterio?
A: Absolutely. For experienced hikers seeking a challenge, Monesterio offers difficult routes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10318612" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro de Tentudia – Monastery of Tentudía loop from Monesterio&lt;/a&gt;. This 16.8 km trail includes a significant ascent to Cerro de Tentudía, the highest point in the province, and typically takes over 5 hours to complete.

Q: Are there any routes that follow historical paths?
A: Yes, the ancient &lt;strong&gt;Vía de la Plata&lt;/strong&gt;, a historic route, passes through the Monesterio area. A popular and gentle section of this route connects Monesterio to Fuente de Cantos, traversing the beautiful Dehesa de Calera de León, offering a blend of history and natural beauty.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter while hiking in Monesterio?
A: The region's diverse habitats, including the dehesas and mountainous areas, support a variety of wildlife. The nearby Sierra Grande de Hornachos, for example, is known for its valuable fauna, especially birds of prey, and is a habitat for the Iberian lynx. While hiking, you might spot various bird species and other local fauna characteristic of the Mediterranean forest and pastureland.

Q: Are there any trails that offer expansive viewpoints?
A: Many trails in Monesterio provide excellent viewpoints. The Sierra del Alto de Aguafría, for instance, features a watchtower at 1,079 meters, offering panoramic vistas. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991577337/alto-de-aguafria-circular-from-the-cruz-del-puerto-recreational-area" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Aguafría – circular from the Cruz del Puerto recreational area&lt;/a&gt; are specifically known for their scenic outlooks.
tips: The Sierra del Alto de Aguafría in Monesterio is an ideal destination for hiking and nature lovers. It is a highly valued route for its natural beauty and the views it offers from the highest points.
If you decide to explore this route, you will be surrounded by nature and breathtaking views. Enjoy your hike! 🏞️🚶‍♂️🥾🚵🌞

The Sierra de Monesterio is a mountainous region located in the foothills of the Sierra Morena, in the province of Badajoz, Extremadura. This area is known for its natural beauty and rich biodiversity.
